clusters.title: ‘One Battle After Another’ Wins Best Picture at 2026 Oscars
clusters.summary: At the 2026 Academy Awards, the film ‘One Battle After Another’ was awarded Best Picture, highlighting its critical acclaim and popularity. Sean Penn, who won the Oscar for Best Supporting Actor for his role in the film, chose not to attend the ceremony. This recognition marks a significant achievement in the film's journey and underscores its impact in the industry.

clusters.title: Trump Seeks Coalition to Secure Strait of Hormuz with Allies
clusters.summary: President Donald Trump has called on approximately seven countries that rely on Middle Eastern oil to join a coalition aimed at policing the Strait of Hormuz. This initiative is part of efforts to ensure maritime security in a region critical for global oil transportation. The request underscores ongoing tensions in the area and the U.S. commitment to maintaining stability.

clusters.title: Trump Threatens to Delay Xi Summit Over Strait of Hormuz Issue
clusters.summary: President Trump has indicated that he may postpone an upcoming summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping if China does not assist in reopening the Strait of Hormuz. This potential delay underscores ongoing tensions between the U.S. and China, particularly regarding international shipping and security concerns in the region. The summit, originally scheduled for two weeks from now, is seen as crucial for addressing trade and diplomatic relations.

clusters.title: Judge Blocks Subpoenas in Investigation of Fed Chair Powell
clusters.summary: A federal judge has quashed subpoenas issued by the Justice Department in its investigation of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. This decision halts the grand jury's efforts to obtain testimony and documents related to Powell's actions, raising questions about the limits of executive accountability. The ruling is significant as it may impact ongoing inquiries into the Federal Reserve's operations and leadership.

clusters.title: Severe Storms Bring Snow, High Winds, and Tornado Threat to US
clusters.summary: A severe weather system is affecting much of the eastern United States, bringing heavy snowfall to the Upper Midwest and high winds across various regions. Thunderstorms are developing in the South, while several mid-Atlantic states face the risk of heavy rain and potential tornadoes. This extreme weather poses significant challenges for communities in the impacted areas.

clusters.title: UK Adds Alcohol-Free Beer to Inflation Measurement Basket
clusters.summary: The UK will include alcohol-free beer in its inflation measurement basket starting next month, reflecting a shift towards healthier living among consumers. Alongside alcohol-free beer, items such as hummus and motorhomes have also been added to better capture changing spending habits and rising costs. This update highlights evolving consumer preferences in the context of the ongoing economic landscape.

items.title: Sobering times: alcohol-free beer added to UK inflation basket
items.description:
Hummus and pet grooming also join list of goods and services used to help judge the impact of rising prices

  • Business live – latest updates

The UK’s increasing sobriety will be recognised from next month in the basket of goods used to calculate inflation after alcohol-free beer was added to a list by the Office for National Statistics totalling 760 items.

Hummus and pet grooming were also included in the list of goods and services used to help judge the impact of rising prices on the cost of living.

Continue reading...

items.title: Alcohol-free beer and houmous used to measure inflation as UK shifts to healthier living
items.description: Houmous and motorhomes are also added to the basket of goods and services used to chart the rising cost of living.

image.title: A hand holding a pint of craft beer against the sky, illustrating a casual outdoor setting.
image.author: Jess Low

clusters.title: Oldest Whale Song Recording Reveals Insights into Communication
clusters.summary: Researchers have uncovered the oldest known recording of a humpback whale song, dating back to 1949, which may enhance understanding of whale communication. This discovery provides valuable insights into the acoustic behaviors of these marine mammals and their social interactions. The findings could have implications for marine biology and conservation efforts.

items.title: Oldest-known whale song recording provides new insight into ocean sounds
items.description:
Recording of humpback whale from 1949 could also provide new understanding of how the huge animals communicate

A haunting whale song discovered on decades-old audio equipment could open up a new understanding of how the huge animals communicate, according to researchers who say it is the oldest such recording known.

The song is that of a humpback whale, a marine giant beloved by whale watchers for its docile nature and spectacular leaps from the water, and was recorded by scientists in March 1949 in Bermuda, said researchers at Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution in Falmouth, Massachusetts.

Continue reading...

items.title: Oldest known whale recording could unlock mysteries of the ocean
items.description: Researchers say the discovery of the oldest known recordings of whale sounds could open up a new understanding of how the huge animals communicate

clusters.summary: Recent attacks on oil export facilities in the Gulf region have led to mixed movements in oil prices, reflecting heightened concerns over supply disruptions. The ongoing conflict in the Middle East is contributing to market volatility, with analysts predicting potential further gains in oil prices if tensions escalate. This situation underscores the fragility of global oil supply chains amid geopolitical unrest.

clusters.title: Trump Confronts Choices as Iran Conflict Enters Third Week
clusters.summary: As the conflict with Iran enters its third week, President Trump is faced with critical decisions regarding the U.S. military strategy. The options on the table include continuing military engagement or potentially declaring victory and withdrawing forces, each carrying significant implications for U.S. foreign policy and regional stability. The evolving situation raises questions about the administration's approach and the long-term consequences of the conflict.

items.title: Entering War’s Third Week, Trump Faces Stark Choices
items.description: As the conflict with Iran expands and intensifies, President Trump’s options — to fight on, or to move toward declaring victory and pulling back — both carry deeply problematic consequences.

items.title: Sunday shows preview: Trump faces questions about length of Iran conflict as war enters third week - The Hill
items.description: Sunday shows preview: Trump faces questions about length of Iran conflict as war enters third week  The Hill

clusters.title: Michigan Synagogue Attacker Linked to Deceased Hezbollah Commander
clusters.summary: The Israeli military has stated that the individual who attacked a synagogue in Michigan is the brother of a deceased Hezbollah commander. This connection raises concerns about potential ties between domestic incidents and international militant organizations. The situation underscores ongoing tensions related to Hezbollah's influence and activities beyond the Middle East.

clusters.title: Orbán and Magyar Hold Rival Rallies Ahead of April Election
clusters.summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán and his challenger, Péter Magyar, held large rallies to demonstrate their political strength ahead of the upcoming April election. The events highlighted the ongoing rivalry between the ruling Fidesz party and the opposition, as both sides seek to mobilize support in a politically charged atmosphere. These rallies are significant as they set the stage for a closely contested electoral battle.

clusters.title: Zelensky Claims Russia Supplies Iran with Drones Targeting US Bases
clusters.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has stated that Russia is supplying Iran with Shahed drones, which are reportedly being used against U.S. military bases. This development raises concerns about the implications for regional security and the ongoing conflict involving multiple nations. The exchange of military technology between Russia and Iran highlights the evolving dynamics of international alliances amid ongoing geopolitical tensions.

clusters.summary: The U.S. is deploying approximately 2,500 Marines aboard the USS Tripoli to the Middle East as part of a response to escalating regional tensions. clusters.title: Fatal US Military Crash Involves Deployed Father and Crew Members
clusters.summary: A recent military aircraft crash resulted in the deaths of several crew members, including an Alabama father who had just been deployed. The incident, which involved personnel from Ohio, has raised concerns about the safety and conditions of military operations. Investigations are underway to determine the cause of the crash and its implications for military readiness.

clusters.title: Kimi Antonelli Secures First F1 Victory in China for Mercedes
clusters.summary: Kimi Antonelli achieved his first Formula 1 victory at the Chinese Grand Prix, leading a one-two finish for the Mercedes team. This win marks a continuation of Mercedes' strong performance in the current season, further solidifying their dominance in the sport. Antonelli's success is seen as a significant milestone in his racing career.

clusters.title: FCC Chair Warns Broadcasters Over Iran War Coverage Standards
clusters.summary: The chair of the Federal Communications Commission has issued a warning to broadcasters regarding their coverage of the ongoing conflict in Iran, suggesting potential repercussions for those who do not adhere to certain standards. This statement comes amid criticism from former President Trump, who has expressed concerns about media reporting on the war. The situation raises questions about the balance between press freedom and regulatory oversight in times of conflict.

clusters.title: Trump Calls for Regional Cooperation to Secure Strait of Hormuz
clusters.summary: President Trump has urged neighboring countries to collaborate in efforts to secure the Strait of Hormuz amid rising tensions with Iran, which has threatened retaliation against its neighbors. The call for action comes as concerns grow over the stability of this crucial maritime passage, vital for global oil shipments. The situation underscores the geopolitical complexities in the region and the potential for escalating conflicts.

clusters.summary: The United States conducted airstrikes on Kharg Island, targeting Iranian military assets amid rising tensions in the region. In retaliation, Iran issued threats against cities in the United Arab Emirates, escalating concerns over security in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical maritime passage. This incident highlights the ongoing conflict between the US and Iran and its potential implications for regional stability.

clusters.title: US Bombs Iranian Military Sites, Trump Threatens Oil Infrastructure
clusters.summary: The United States has conducted airstrikes on military sites located on an island crucial to Iran's oil network. President Trump issued a warning that further actions could target the island's oil infrastructure if Iran continues to disrupt maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz. This escalation highlights ongoing tensions between the U.S. and Iran over regional security and oil transportation.

items.title: The Latest: Trump threatens Iran's oil infrastructure after US bombs island military sites
items.description: President Donald Trump says the U.S. destroyed military sites on an island vital to Iran’s oil network and warned that the island’s oil infrastructure could be next if Iran continues to interfere with the passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz

clusters.summary: Eight individuals with alleged ties to antifa have been convicted on terrorism charges related to a shooting incident at a Texas immigration facility. clusters.summary: The Justice Department has charged a man for allegedly selling a firearm to the individual responsible for a shooting at Old Dominion University, which resulted in one death and two injuries. clusters.title: Ric Grenell Steps Down as Kennedy Center Executive Director
clusters.summary: Ric Grenell has announced his resignation as the executive director of the Kennedy Center, a position he has held since his appointment by former President Trump. The move comes as Trump prepares to appoint a new leader for the prestigious arts institution, which plays a significant role in American culture and the arts. This transition marks a notable change in leadership at the Kennedy Center amid ongoing discussions about its direction and priorities.

clusters.title: Fourth Quarter GDP Growth Revised Down to 0.7%
clusters.summary: The U.S. economy's growth for the fourth quarter has been revised down to 0.7%, indicating a slower pace than previously reported. This adjustment comes amid ongoing concerns about inflation, with January's core inflation rate recorded at 3.1%. The revision highlights potential challenges for economic recovery and policymaking moving forward.
